payload.reason: Victor updated Builder Console task status with grounded evidence and governance approval.

request.taskTitle: Introduce canonical UOR address type and BLAKE3 content addressing
request.query: What should happen next with the governed automation task "Introduce canonical UOR address type and BLAKE3 content addressing" in phase "UOR Content-Addressed Identity Migration"?

groundedContext.query: What should happen next with the governed automation task "Introduce canonical UOR address type and BLAKE3 content addressing" in phase "UOR Content-Addressed Identity Migration"?
semanticNodeLabels: Introduce canonical UOR address type and BLAKE3 content addressing
Content-addressed identity uses the designated BLAKE3 pipeline.
Replace ad hoc Victor memory identity with deterministic UOR addressing so memory writes, lookups, deduplication, and supersession lineage are cryptographically and semantically stable.
Victor memory supports a canonical UOR address type.
Rewire memory, tier, and graph structures onto UOR identity
Core memory and tier structures key on UOR identity.
recommendedNextActions: Implement existing SHA-based integrity paths remain backward-compatible during migration

before.title: Introduce canonical UOR address type and BLAKE3 content addressing
before.description: Add deterministic content-addressed identity primitives so Victor memory artifacts can derive stable UOR addresses from canonical bytes instead of relying on UUIDs and duplicate hash pipelines.
acceptance: Victor memory supports a canonical UOR address type.
Content-addressed identity uses the designated BLAKE3 pipeline.
Existing SHA-based integrity paths remain backward-compatible during migration.
